old_claim.note: Agriculture provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The review finds early differentiation among both hunter-gatherers and farmers, so agriculture is not a universal prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract: surveys hunter-gatherers and farmers in Neolithic western Eurasia 11,700-5,300 years ago, finding elevated wealth inequality was possible but usually rare and ephemeral.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Neolithic western Eurasian hunter-gatherers and farmers sometimes exhibited early, usually ephemeral wealth differentiation.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The scoped evidence establishes the milestone without the removed dependency. The review finds early differentiation among both hunter-gatherers and farmers, so agriculture is not a universal prerequisite.

ontology_before: social_stratification treated agriculture as a direct causal prerequisite.
ontology_after: social_stratification is scoped to the directly evidenced mechanism and no longer claims agriculture as a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-12-structural-accuracy-batch: social_stratification no longer directly depends on agriculture.
would_reject_if: A reliable source demonstrates that every implementation within the current social_stratification scope technically requires agriculture.
short_reason: The review finds early differentiation among both hunter-gatherers and farmers, so agriculture is not a universal prerequisite.
